write_file({"path": "internal/language_tiers/language_tiers.go", "contents": "package language_tiers\n\nimport \"github.com/iksnae/code-certification/internal/analysis\"\n\n// Tier represents the level of tooling support for a language.\ntype Tier int\n\nconst (\n\t// TierGeneric covers unregistered languages with no custom analyzer.\n\tTierGeneric Tier = iota\n\t// TierFull covers languages with a registered analyzer providing full\n\t// parse/introspect/certify capabilities.\n\tTierFull\n)\n\n// TierForLanguage returns the Tier for the given language identifier.\n// It delegates to analysis.ForLanguage: if a custom analyzer is registered\n// the language earns TierFull; otherwise TierGeneric.\nfunc TierForLanguage(lang string) Tier {\n\tif analysis.ForLanguage(lang) != nil {\n\t\treturn TierFull\n\t}\n\treturn TierGeneric\n}\n"}) → wrote internal/language_tiers/language_tiers.go (726 bytes)
write_file({"contents": "package language_tiers\n\nimport \"testing\"\n\nfunc TestTierForLanguage(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname string\n\t\tlang string\n\t\twant Tier\n\t}{\n\t\t// Registered languages → TierFull\n\t\t{name: \"go\", lang: \"go\", want: TierFull},\n\t\t{name: \"ts\", lang: \"ts\", want: TierFull},\n\t\t{name: \"py\", lang: \"py\", want: TierFull},\n\t\t{name: \"rs\", lang: \"rs\", want: TierFull},\n\t\t{name: \"js\", lang: \"js\", want: TierFull},\n\n\t\t// Unregistered languages → TierGeneric\n\t\t{name: \"ruby\", lang: \"ruby\", want: TierGeneric},\n\t\t{name: \"swift\", lang: \"swift\", want: TierGeneric},\n\t\t{name: \"c\", lang: \"c\", want: TierGeneric},\n\t\t{name: \"empty\", lang: \"\", want: TierGeneric},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tgot := TierForLanguage(tt.lang)\n\t\t\tif got != tt.want {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"TierForLanguage(%q) = %v, want %v\", tt.lang, got, tt.want)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n", "path": "internal/language_tiers/language_tiers_test.go"}) → wrote internal/language_tiers/language_tiers_test.go (865 bytes)

// Package analysis provides language-agnostic code analysis through a unified interface.
// Each language implements the Analyzer interface, producing consistent structural
// metrics that feed into the certification scoring pipeline.
package analysis

import (
	"fmt"
	"time"

	"github.com/iksnae/code-certification/internal/domain"
)

// SymbolKind identifies the type of a discovered code symbol.
type SymbolKind int

const (
	SymbolFunction  SymbolKind = iota // Standalone function
	SymbolMethod                      // Method on a type/class
	SymbolClass                       // Type, class, struct, interface
	SymbolConstant                    // Exported constant or variable
	SymbolInterface                   // Interface (separate from class for languages that distinguish)
)

var symbolKindStrings = map[SymbolKind]string{
	SymbolFunction:  "function",
	SymbolMethod:    "method",
	SymbolClass:     "class",
	SymbolConstant:  "constant",
	SymbolInterface: "interface",
}

func (k SymbolKind) String() string {
	if s, ok := symbolKindStrings[k]; ok {
		return s
	}
	return fmt.Sprintf("SymbolKind(%d)", k)
}

// Symbol represents a discovered code unit within a source file.
type Symbol struct {
	Name      string     // Symbol name
	Kind      SymbolKind // Function, method, class, etc.
	StartLine int        // 1-indexed start line
	EndLine   int        // 1-indexed end line (inclusive)
	Parent    string     // Enclosing type/class name (empty for top-level)
	Exported  bool       // Whether the symbol is exported/public
}

// Metrics holds language-agnostic structural analysis results for a code unit.
type Metrics struct {
